## Lab brief

Analyse State of the Union speeches to test whether presidential language predicts
economic outcomes. The exercise applies Boolean search, topic modelling, and
sentiment analysis to the same corpus, building a comparative view of supervised
versus unsupervised text methods.

You have been hired as an analyst for a presidential campaign. The team speculates
that highly positive speeches correlate with stronger economic performance. Your job
is to assess this hypothesis using a dataset of State of the Union speeches
(1980-2017) matched with annual GDP growth rates.

The SOTU corpus is publicly available (e.g., on Kaggle). You are free to analyse
the full dataset or a justified subset.

## Tasks

1. Describe in words how Boolean search combined with text regression could address
   the campaign team's hypothesis. Discuss strengths and weaknesses.
2. Define a set of positive search terms and count their frequency in each speech.
   Normalise by document length.
3. Run at least five Boolean search specifications with different term sets. Regress
   term frequency on GDP growth for each specification. Compare the results.
4. Assess how sensitive the regression output is to the choice of terms. Would you
   trust a single specification? Why or why not?
5. Preprocess the corpus (tokenise, remove stop-words, lemmatise) and fit an LDA
   topic model. Justify your choice of the number of topics.
6. Visualise the topics using word clouds or ranked word lists. Can you associate
   meaningful themes with each topic?
7. Analyse how topic prevalence varies across presidents. Illustrate with
   appropriate figures.
8. Correlate topic weights with GDP growth. Compare the explanatory power of the
   topic model with the Boolean search approach.
9. Extract the first paragraph of each speech. Score sentiment manually (-1 to 1)
   and with an automated dictionary (e.g., VADER). Compare the two scores.

## Starter outline

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import statsmodels.api as sm
from sklearn.decomposition import LatentDirichletAllocation
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer
from scipy.stats import pearsonr
```

Deliver answers in a PDF report (maximum 5 pages). Clearly label each task.
Figures and tables must be referenced in the text.
